mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, prompting the Federal Reserve to delay rate cuts and compress equity risk premiums, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, triggering recession fears that drive equity sell-offs over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged corporate sectors, causing earnings revisions to turn negative and depressing equity prices over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month while GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in >55% of triggered periods, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands corporate revenue bases and improves profit margins, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth remains positive while S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 150500.0 for three consecutive months, robust hiring validates economic resilience, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal triggers positive 20-day forward returns in less than 45% of historical instances, or the average forward return during triggered periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals supply-side efficiency outpacing monetary drag, compressing risk premiums and generating positive equity returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During high-inflation regimes, rising consumer prices erode real disposable income and suppress discretionary spending, causing retail and consumer discretionary earnings to miss estimates and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPI exceeds 3.0% YoY, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline momentum model.
